game.title: Work-Life Balance
game.short_text: Can you juggle two mini-games simultaneously? Balance work tasks and daily life under time pressure!

details.title: Work-Life Balance
details.description:
You juggle two jobs and a whirlwind of daily life tasks. Each day, you must tackle one work task and one life task — simultaneously!Find your balance and complete all tasks across 4 demanding days to maximize your score and prove you can handle it all.Can you beat my high score of 30'599?
This is my submission to Gamedev.js Jam 2025!
Challenges I participated for this jam:

Open Source Challenge by GitHub: Code is available on GitHub.
Build it with Phaser Challenge by Phaser Studio: Phaser framework was used to create this game.
Controls
Mouse and Touch
How to Play
For 4 days, you must juggle two roles—Accountant and Editor—while handling daily life tasks: Dancing Class and Lawn Mowing. Each day, complete one work and one life task simultaneously within 60 seconds.
Life tasks earn you points.Work tasks apply a multiplier to those points.
Balance your focus to maximize both!
Work Tasks
Accountant: Add cash and inventory to calculate total assets. Pick the correct answer from four options.Editor: Spot the typo among four words.
Life Tasks
Dancing Class: Follow your teacher’s moves by raising your left or right hand up or down as shown in the speech bubble.Lawn Mowing: Steer your mower through the backyard by turning clockwise or anticlockwise to cut as much grass as possible.
Credits and Tools
Special thanks to my support and inspiration at home!
Thanks to my play testersGitHub Page
Code: Home made typescript spaghetti code using WebStorm and bundled with vite.jsGraphics: Inspired by AI, hand drawn by me in AsepriteMusic: All by myself using Reaper, Focusrite Scarlett 2i2 interface, a ALESIS Q49 midi keyboard, Decent Sampler plugin, Soviet PIF Synth, Pocket Operators PO-32 tonic and PO-14 subSound effects: My Bosch PMF 220 CE as lawn mower (making off) and sounds from my Pocket Operators (PO-32 tonic and PO-14 sub) and jsfxrFramework: Phaser 3
Fonts: Luckiest Guy, Nunito and Special Elite from Google Fonts

details.title: Split Push
details.description:
Split Push
        
        
            Push boxes, solve puzzles and shift the Balance! Push the boxes onto the targets. But be careful! Moving between the two game windows shifts the balance of the game.
        
        
            
        
        Context
        
            This game was created in about a week for Gamedev.js Jam 2025 on the theme "Balance". The game uses the engine Phaser 3 and is written in TypeScript.
        
        Controls
        
            Arrow Keys or WASD: Move the character.
            R: Restart the current level.U: Undo last move.
        Assets used
        
            Music BRAIN TEASER 3 by Eric Matyas - www.soundimage.org
            All the visuals and sound effects were created by me.
        Source codeThe source code is available on my GitHub: https://github.com/CASTEL-Florian/SplitPush-GameDevJs2025Gameplay videoIf you want to see the solution of the puzzles, watch the gameplay video!

resultData.title: Sloshed: A long way to bed

game.title: Sloshed: A long way to bed
game.short_text: Find your way to the bed without falling, standing still is also a good idea

details.title: Sloshed: A long way to bed
details.description:
Sloshed: A Long Way to Bed
You've made it home, somehow. 
But something feels off...
Venture depeer and deeper into your subconcious in this movement-based rougelike experience.
 Your goal? Reach your bed. Your worst enemy? Yourself.
 
Scattered across your apartment are powerups to help you along the way and entities of dubious nature.
The more you move, the more disoriented you become. Drift in random directions, fight to stay upright, and avoid the painful shame of faceplanting into your coffee table. Stand still to regain focus — or hit Shift to sprint like a maniac... but be warned: it might just be the last thing you do (upright).

Features
A drunk physics-based character controller
Full-body tilt and drift based on your intoxication
Wiggly camera, wobbly legs, questionable decisions
Simple goal: reach the bed — if you can
Controls
WASD — Move (kind of)
Shift — Run (not recommended)
Space — Dive (aim for the bed!) 
E— Use/Interact
Powerups
Water — Clears your head a bitHoly Water — Useful to banish evil spirits (use with E!)Getter Upper — Falling might not be the end anymore, for 1 time that isGuide Light — Lights up the way to bedFlashlight — To actually see where you're goingSpeed demon — Move faster than you should
Sticky feet — Helps against some enemies but makes you move slower
Dizzimeter — See how close you are to falling face first into the groundPowerup counter — See how many powerups are left to find in the level
Enemies
Hoover— Watch out for the nasty robot vacuum, might make you tripHunter— A weird entity that might appear in your subconcious, be sure to reach the bed before it catches you!
Made for Gamedev.js Jam 2025 by Fisho & Willyx – Theme: "Balance"

game.title: Playtesters and Co.
game.short_text: Balance, A game dev's worst enemy.

details.title: Playtesters and Co.
details.description:
Welcome to Playtesters and Co.

You have been selected to join the Balancing Program. Your assignment is to test five internal game builds, adjusting their difficulty parameters to achieve optimal balance. Your performance will be assessed based on accuracy and efficiency. A final grade will be issued upon program completion.
 
Proceed carefully. All decisions are final. 

Note: The black screen after pressing play means the game is loading. Please be patient. For the best experience, play at 100% browser scale.

Jam
Placed #3 for Community rankings and #1 In Graphics
This game was made in 8 days for Gamedev.js Jam 2025 
Theme - Balance
Our Take – We approached the theme a little differently, focusing on the game development meaning of "balance" — tweaking numbers, mechanics, and difficulty to create the best player experience.

Team
Kitongo - Game Art
SeeOne - Programming + UI Art
Razorflip - Music

Resources
Everything except the SFX was made by us.

Future plans
- Expanded Storyline
- New games to balance
- Better endings

game.title: Balance FISH
game.short_text: Balance the grid, dive deep, and rescue the city! A puzzle adventure about balancing electricity.

details.title: Balance FISH
details.description:
Balance FISH
Balance the Grid, Save the City!

As a deep-sea fish (with legs), restore balance to the power grid to bring your city back to life!
Star on GithubVisit my website
* * *
Quick Info
📱 Playable on mobile devices💻 Made for the Gamedev.js Jam 2025
Overview
Plug the cables into the substations to carry electricity further into the depths. Will the strange sea creatures help or hinder your quest to restore balance? ⚡
Controls
🌊 Hold W or UP to swim forward (or walk)🐟 Press the LEFT or RIGHT keys (A or D) to rotate🔁 Hold down R to restart the level💥 Hold X to balance or detach cables
Balance by Name, FISH by Nature
Humanity knows very little about the depths of the ocean. But today a mysterious creature emerged from its depths: the Balance Fish.
It's a deeply unfortunate moment in the deep.
Your underwater city's power supply has become unbalanced. Embark on a mission to harness energy from the Nuclear Reactor, balance the grid, and save the city! ⚡
Developer Notes 
Hello there! I'm Hope41 👋
Here's some additional info about Balance FISH...
Made in pure JavaScript, no libraries
Balance FISH was made in 13 days for the Gamedev.js Jam 2025
No Artificial Intelligence was used to create this game
Music made on a whim one evening, when I realised the game might suffer without some noise to hum along to 🎶
All sound effects sourced from authentic Pixabay sounds

They say it's the weird games that get noticed. I'm not sure that's true - but hey, here we are anyway — with a strange, deep-sea fish (with legs, don't forget), trying to balance an electrical system to bring his underwater city back to life.
* * *
More games at https://joachimford.uk!Follow me on GitHub!
* * *
I hope you enjoy playing Balance FISH!!Please share your own thoughts in the comments below ~

moreInfo.Author: Hope41
moreInfo.Tags: 2D, Math, No AI

game.title: SUPER-SOBER
game.short_text: A game by Slim. For the Gamedevjs Jam 2025. Theme: Balance.

details.title: SUPER-SOBER
details.description:
SUPER-SOBER is a game by Slim. Made for the Gamedevjs Jam 2025. Theme: Balance.
Balance the character with drunk controls and try to reach home.
Inspired by an old Flash game I played when I was younger. Also inspired by games like Getting Over It and QWOP.
The player will face different obstacles, characters and two whole directions to explore. Learn with each attempt and unlock different endings. Challenge yourself with the easy to understand, but hard to master drunk controls.
Features a whole new way to control the character with controls made from the ground up. 
Made with Godot 4.4.
Credits:Road and Bus stop by https://elbolilloduro.itch.ioMain title music by https://opengameart.org/content/tragic-ambient-main-menuEDIT: The game turned out to be a little harder than expected on the Web Player version (because of complex stuff with the mouse movement).The game is completely beatable on both versions. But, the Windows Version is the one that was playtested the most, and represents better the level of difficulty that was intended.Thank you for playing this mess!

game.title: This game is unbalanced!
game.short_text: The game where YOU balance the game!

details.title: This game is unbalanced!
details.description:
Game developers know that: it's hard to balance a game.
You create your nice game and your playtesters just say : "This game is unbalanced!".They all have their specific desires and their own concept of what a balanced game is... It's so hard to satisfy the players!
In "This game is unbalanced!", you watch a little monster play some games. Unfortunately they are all unbalanced for this little fella: either too easy or too difficult... So your goal is to balance them!
-----

A game created for GamedevJS 2024.Theme: BALANCE
-----
Challenges

Open Source ChallengeSource code is published here: https://github.com/adrien-gueret/this-game-is-unbalanced
Build it with Phaser ChallengeThis game is built with Phaser (even though I had never used the library before!It's my first game with it, I'm sure there are a lot of weird things in my code :D )$NOODS ChallengeGoal was to vibe code a Phaser game: I've heavily used Copilot and its agent mode to build this project. Learn more about that below.
-----

Credits
GraphismsBackgrounds generated via ChatGPTSpritesheets create by myself on good-old Paint :)  AudioMusic from SUNO AISome sounds effect from KenneyOther sounds from jsfxrOne or two jingle effects were stored on my computer since quite a time now: I unfortunately don't remember where I get them ^^'Game conceptBy myselfCodeUsage of Phaser library Around 40% of the code written by myselfAround 60% of the code written by Copilot (see below to know more!)
-----

Vibe coding? Is it worth it?
In order to create "This game is unbalanced!", my partner in crime was copilot. This game has been built in less than 13 days with Phaser... while I have 0 knowledge on Phaser.
I've started asking Copilot to create a template for a Phaser project: it did a good job with importing correctly the library and creating a basic project architecture.
Then I told Copilot my idea, with more or less precise specs, and it started to create Phaser scenes by itself! The result was quite impressive 'cause it was more or less what I had in mind. It was ugly, for sure, but it was a very good base for my game!
The next steps were quite easy: giving more instructions to Copilot, or very specific Phaser stuff. I've asked him how to add my own graphics, how to add spritesheets, how to animate them and so on. This step was the most important one: I've tried to understand the code it wrote for me, and I asked for some explanations when needed.
Honestly, thanks to that, I've learned how to write a Phaser game without reading any docs nor tutorials! Quite impressive.
At first my goal was only to balance a platform game but, since vibe coding a Phaser game was fun and interesting, I wanted to go even further. What if I could code more types of games?
That's why "This game is unbalanced!" also have RPG-like fighting levels. Like the platforms ones, I've tried to understand how the generated code works, and I did a lot of refactors to make it better (well... better in my opinion at least!).
Two types of game weren't enough, so I've decided to vibe-code a third one: a racing game, ala "Super Mario Kart"! But it was a bit too ambitious... Impossible for Copilot to generate a Mode 7 effect, and I could not find any tutorials to create one by myself. I was a bit surprised, since Phaser seems to be very good for any 2D games!
So I gave up on the racing game and I switched to Match 3. The deadline  was approaching so I didn't refactor any of Copilot code on this one. The Match 3 Simulation file is probably the most hideous one on this project, with a lot of dead comments Frenglish stuff between almost 1500 lines... ^^'
But anyway.
It was very fun, and I thank the Gamedev.js organizers to encourage us to test vibe coding. 
Before the jam I didn't know Phaser at all. But I was able to submit a full game built with it and containing 3 different types of mini-games.And I learnt how to use Phaser.
All thanks to Copilot.
Just wow. 
--
To balance a bit this return of experience (hey, it is the theme of the gamejam after all!) please note I am a web developer for years now, I know how to code, I know good patterns and I can detect when the AI is trying to bullshit me.
I am not sure the tool will be that efficient for a junior developer. It will certainly help it to start quicker, but without being able to understand the generated code it can be more harmful than helpful, I guess (just look at the Match 3 Simulation file, it's a total mess!).
If the user of the AI has the goal to learn and understand what the AI is generating, it could be the best educational support ever made.
But if its goal is to only click "accept" on everything the AI suggests in order to develop an app whatever the code quality. It could be the most efficient unmaintainable code generator ever made :)
--
Oopsy, I've gone a bit beyond the scope of the game description. I may write an article about that one day! If so I'll put the link here.Edit: article written! You can read it here: https://medium.com/openclassrooms-produit-design-et-ing%C3%A9nierie/comment-jai-...(in french, sorry for that!)
Thanks for reading — I hope you had fun with my first Phaser game!

resultData.title: Do ducks dream of algorithmic worldgen

game.title: Do ducks dream of algorithmic worldgen
game.short_text: Game about a duck stuck in an ever-changing dream

details.title: Do ducks dream of algorithmic worldgen
details.description:
The duck wants to sleep.
Journey through the ever-changing archipelago in an attempt to turn the world into night. Collect resources to bridge the gap between islands and make a wish to turn the balance of the world from islands to space.

Controls:
WASD/Arrow Keys - Movement
Space/X - Interact

How to play:
Move around the islands collecting items by moving into them.
Create bridges between islands with interact button.

Wood is used to make bridges over water.
Stone is used to make bridges over lava.
Crystals are used to make bridges over sky.
Snowballs are traded for crystals by snowmen.
Collect coins from chests, shipwrecks and buried treasure.
Coins are used to transform whole islands through the monuments or wells.

Avoid getting hit by the mysterious void tornados.
Inventory is shown on top of the character.
When enough of the world is turned into space, a bed might appear.
Game by: Noel, Miotismon & Overlite

details.title: balaline
details.description:
balaline is a puzzle-survival game with the goal to complete as many levels as possible within the time limit.
You control a line which separates two sides. Every level consists of randomly placed numbers. The goal in each level is to divide the numbers in a way that the sum is the same on both sides. 
If you manage to do it, you get a few seconds additional time. If there is a difference you lose the amount in seconds.
The amount of time you gain or lose is affected by how quick you make your decision. The longer you take the less points or the more damage you get.
Numbers that lay on the line won't be counted. You can use this to find unique solutions.
Controls
mouse to move the linemousewheel to rotate the lineHold shift to rotate fasterHold ctrl to rotate slowerLeft mouse button or space to confirm your choice
Note: It might not always be possible to perfectly divide the numbers, so you need to find the balance between looking for the solution and accepting a penalty.

game.title: Time to Panic!
game.short_text: An intentionally frustrating tiny platformer thing with cute, colorful pixelart to maximize the pain.

details.title: Time to Panic!
details.description:
Time to Panic! is a short platformer with intentionally slippery and frustrating controls. You play as some random guy whose bank account has been hacked, and you must stop the hacker before your bank account goes dry. Why? If the balance of your account hits zero, then the bank refuses to revive you if you die. Yes, that is perfectly cromulent logic.
The game was made within 10 days for Gamedev.js Jam 2025, so don't expect miracles, 'cause there sure ain't any.

Controls:
(Gamepad controls inside parentheses)
Arrow keys/WASD (dpad/left stick): MoveSpace/Z/J/left mouse button (button A): Jump (press twice to double jump)Escape/Enter (uh... that menu button thing on modern Xbox pads): pauseEnter/Space (button A or  that menu button thing described above): accept stuff in the menus.


Source code:
Here: https://github.com/jani-nykanen/time-to-panic

details.title: Feed The Demon
details.description:
Submission for Gamedev.js Jam 2025. Theme: Balance.

Collect food while keeping your deeds balanced, then feed the demon!
Controls:
W/A/S/D to move around.Left Mouse Click to attack.Space to feed the demon (only work at farm).E to interact.P to pause and or open game menu.
Notes:
Please consider letting me know if you encounter any errors, bugs, or inconveniences.
External Assets:
Music: SVL's RPG Music Pack by Shononoki.Font: monogram by datagoblin

game.title: Balance'em
game.short_text: Game that you balance for yourself

details.title: Balance'em
details.description:
This game is a merge of inventory managment dungeon-crawler with innovative system of personal balance. 
How to play? 





You enter a dungeon to beat bosses. To kill a boss you have to deplete one of 4 his stats: health, strength, agility or morale. You have a backpack with differen weird staff to deal damage or restore your own stats.
But what about the balance? Before each battle you set a score of stars. They affect bosses might and list of abilities. In the end it is up to you to collect as many stars from defeated bosses and run through the dungeon
Claim your fame!
Music - Augmentality (Brandon Morris) Creepy Forest (F)
Tools:
Unity, Aseprite
It is made by:

filgreen3 code/gamedesign/original ideape4Q07 art/gamedesignliberato code/gamedesign

moreInfo.Rating: Rated 4.4 out of 5 stars(16 total ratings)

game.title: Balance Ma'atters
game.short_text: Play cards, balance scales, defeat chaos.

details.title: Balance Ma'atters
details.description:
Balance Ma’atters
The world’s out of balance. Again. As Ma’at, the goddess of truth and order, it’s up to you to fix the mess Apophis made — with nothing but a handful of cards.
In this quick and quirky card game, you’ll battle mischievous beasts, tip scales, and unleash massive attacks when balance is restored. Just be careful: a little tilt is fine, but if you fully tip those scales... well, chaos has a lot of free time.

🕹️ How to Play:
  Play cards to attack, defend, and shift the scales.Balance small scales to gain extra multipliers.Balance the large scale to unleash stored power. At the end of your turn, if a small scale is fully unbalanced, Ma’at will skip her next turn to recover.At the end of your turn, if the large scale is fully unbalanced, you immediately lose the battle.
 Stay calm. Stay steady. Save the world.
⚡ Made for Gamedev.js jam 2025!
Balance Ma’atters is a small, light-hearted project with a touch of roguelite.  Some fancy features didn’t make it this time, but hey, balance first, sparkle later. Thanks for playing and helping keep the universe scales steady!
©️ Credit
Equip Sound Effect by Homemade_SFX from PixabayHammer Fixing Sound Effect by freesound_community from PixabayError Sound Effect by Universfield from PixabayHit Sound Effect by Dennis from Pixabay

game.title: BURDEN
game.short_text: Avoid the traps of the Chained Queen

details.title: BURDEN
details.description:
You are the last man standing against the Chained Queen.
Will you be able to avoid all her traps and triumph over her trials?
In BURDEN, gather your courage and advance step by step through the Chained Queen's kingdom. In this retro-styled, chess-inspired game, you'll have to observe your surroundings, plan your moves, and capture all your opponent's pawns if you hope to survive.
CONTROLS :
- Right click in the left part of the game to move the camera and observe your surroundings.
- Left click to drag and drop actions into your timeline.
Press the start button to start your turn. But be aware! Your number of turn per level is limited!

moreInfo.Author: KlimoGD
moreInfo.Tags: Chess, Dark Fantasy, Dungeon Crawler, FPS, Gothic, Low-poly, PSX (PlayStation), Turn-based

details.title: Jazz Jazz Revolution
details.description:
🎷 Jazz Jazz Revolution
Feel the rhythm...
Balance the sections...
Hold the music together...
Finish the song.

Step into Jazz Jazz Revolution, a fast-paced rhythm game where keeping the band in sync is just as important as hitting the notes.
Each lane represents a different section of the ensemble — strings, brass, piano, drums, bass— and each has its own balance to maintain.
Perfect timing keeps the group stable. Mistakes throw them out of rhythm, risking a complete collapse.
Survive the full performance by balancing precision, timing, and focus as the music grows more intense.
Every note matters. Every group counts.
Hold the band together — or watch the music fall apart.

Play Jazz Jazz Revolution and see if you can master the performance.

🎵 Team

    Coding: Redfeng & 0alx0
    Art: Megan Lu & Sophie Li
    Audio: Yu

game.title: Ticket to Elsewhere
game.short_text: What will you place on the scales?

details.title: Ticket to Elsewhere
details.description:
"If this is a dream, it's the weirdest dream I've ever had..."
Ticket to Elsewhere is a short narrative game about judging the life of someone who's passing through on their way to elsewhere, featuring the voice of Marty Vite. It was made in 13 days for the 2025 Gamedev.js Jam, and inspired by our previous title, An Oceanside Journey on the Elsewhere Express.ControlsThe game is entirely mouse-driven. Click-and-drag factors to the scale to weigh them, or to the Petitioner's portrait to ask about them.Just extract the .zip and play the .exe!

moreInfo.Authors: Team ElseWare, themusingowl, Tintenseher (they/them), swordofdestiny, CynderStorm, barryk013, Moustronaut
moreInfo.Tags: 2D, investigative, Narrative, No AI, Relaxing, Short, Singleplayer, Trains
